title: Fetal Growth Restriction
chief_complaint: I'm 32 weeks pregnant. My fundal height hasn't grown in 3 weeks. My blood pressure is slightly elevated. The baby is moving less than before.

physical_exam.Obstetric: Fundal height 26cm (lagging 3 weeks behind dates). Fetal HR 130. Mildly tender uterus.
physical_exam.General: No headache, no visual changes, no RUQ pain.

hidden_data.ob_us: Fetal biometry: EFW 1,400g (<3rd percentile - severe IUGR). Abdominal circumference <3rd percentile. Head circumference 25th percentile (asymmetric IUGR - brain sparing).
hidden_data.dopplers: Umbilical artery: Absent end-diastolic flow (AEDF - ominous). Middle cerebral artery: Decreased pulsatility index (brain sparing - MCA vasodilation). Ductus venosus: Reversed a-wave (ominous - acidemia, impending demise). Uterine artery: Bilateral notching (uteroplacental insufficiency).
hidden_data.bpp: BPP 4/8 (NST non-reactive, absent fetal breathing, normal tone, normal AFI - score <6 requires action)
hidden_data.cmp: Uric Acid 7.5 (elevated - preeclampsia), Cr 1.0, AST 25, Platelets 200,000. Proteinuria 1+.

correct_diagnosis: Severe Early-Onset Fetal Growth Restriction with Preeclampsia, AEDF, and Brain Sparing - Impending Fetal Demise
key_learning_points: Fetal growth restriction (FGR) = EFW <10th percentile (or <3rd for severe). NOT same as SGA (constitutionally small - normal Dopplers, normal growth velocity). FGR = pathologic placental insufficiency

Etiology: PLACENTAL INSUFFICIENCY (most common - preeclampsia, HTN, thrombophilias, smoking, cocaine). Fetal: Genetic (trisomies 13/18, triploidy), Congenital infection (CMV, toxoplasmosis, Zika), Structural anomalies. Maternal: Undernutrition, Chronic disease (CKD, SLE, cyanotic heart disease)

Classification: Early-onset (<32 weeks): Severe placental dysfunction, abnormal Dopplers, often preeclampsia. Late-onset (>32 weeks): Milder, normal Dopplers, more common. Symmetric (head + abdomen both small - genetic, infection, early insult) vs Asymmetric (head normal, abdomen small - brain sparing from uteroplacental insufficiency in 3rd trimester)

Doppler surveillance: Umbilical artery: Increased resistance → Absent end-diastolic flow → Reversed end-diastolic flow (REDF) = imminent demise (delivery indicated at >32 weeks, <32 weeks - balance prematurity vs stillbirth). MCA Doppler: Decreased PI = brain sparing. Ductus venosus: Reversed a-wave = acidemia (delivery regardless of gestational age). Uterine artery notching = uteroplacental insufficiency

Management: NO treatment to reverse FGR. Only management: TIMING OF DELIVERY. Surveillance: Twice-weekly BPP/NST + weekly Dopplers. Betamethasone 24-34 weeks for fetal lung maturity. Delivery indications: Reversed UA flow >32 weeks, AEDF >34 weeks, Reversed DV a-wave (any GA), Non-reassuring fetal status (BPP <6, recurrent decelerations), Severe preeclampsia, Failure of fetal growth over 2-4 weeks. C-section for standard obstetric indications. Neonatology consult for anticipated preterm delivery. Magnesium sulfate for neuroprotection <32 weeks
